page: Vultr voc-g-1c-4gb-30s-amd is an entry-level virtual machine within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features a single shared AMD vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory. Storage is provided via a local 30 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The single-core AMD processor operates with a shared CPU allocation and a single thread per core. Notably, advanced CPU instruction sets and hardware-accelerated flags, such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, AES cryptography extensions, and virtualization extensions like SVM or VMX, are not available on this instance. This lack of specialized instruction sets limits its capability for cryptographic, vector-heavy, or nested virtualization workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and modest resource footprint position this server as a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads. It is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, development environments, and simple background tasks that do not require sustained CPU performance or advanced hardware acceleration.

It includes 30 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. The single-core CPU operates on a shared allocation model with one thread per core, and lacks advanced hardware instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, and AES cryptography extensions. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for low-intensity workloads. The lack of advanced vector and cryptographic instructions limits its utility for high-performance computing, but it remains highly efficient for simple utility tasks, basic web serving, and development environments.
